questions.text: What is the main warning of Surah Az-Zukhruf about worldly luxury (zukhruf)?
questions.answer: That worldly decoration can deceive people, but it has no value compared to faith and the Hereafter.

questions.text: Why does the Surah highlight that the Quran is 'clear' and in Arabic?
questions.answer: So people can understand it, reflect, and have no excuse for rejecting guidance.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the 'Umm al-Kitab' with Allah?
questions.answer: That revelation has a lofty, preserved source with Allah, showing the Quran's honor and truth.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that people should follow religion mainly because ancestors did so.
questions.answer: False. It criticizes blind following of ancestors without proof.

questions.text: What is one spiritual danger of inherited traditions when they conflict with revelation?
questions.answer: They can become excuses that block truth and keep people trapped in shirk and arrogance.

questions.text: What does Ibrahim (AS) model in his rejection of idols?
questions.answer: Clear dissociation from shirk and loyalty to the Creator alone.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach gratitude when using vehicles or animals for travel?
questions.answer: By remembering Allah who subjected them and acknowledging we could not control them without His gift.

questions.text: Why is the du'a mentioned for riding spiritually important today?
questions.answer: It protects from arrogance, reminds us of dependence on Allah, and turns routine travel into worship.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about people who demand revelation be given to 'important' worldly figures?
questions.answer: It exposes their arrogance; Allah chooses whom He wills for mercy and prophethood.

questions.text: How does the Surah correct the belief that wealth equals honor with Allah?
questions.answer: It shows Allah could have given extreme luxury to disbelievers, but it would only be a test, not proof of honor.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests Allah loves people most when He gives them the most luxury.
questions.answer: False. Worldly luxury is not a sign of love; taqwa and faith are the true measure.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about turning away from Allah's remembrance?
questions.answer: A devil may be assigned as a constant companion, influencing thoughts and choices.

questions.text: What is one warning about bad companionship in this Surah?
questions.answer: A misleading companion can normalize sin, and on Judgment Day that companionship becomes regret and hatred.

questions.text: What will a person wish regarding their devil companion in the Hereafter?
questions.answer: They will wish for extreme distance from them, realizing how harmful the companionship was.

questions.text: How does Surah Az-Zukhruf teach the danger of 'spiritual blindness'?
questions.answer: A person can be guided by deception so they think they are right, until truth becomes undeniable.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Pharaoh's propaganda and pride?
questions.answer: He used worldly power and rivers as proof of greatness, but his arrogance led to destruction.

questions.text: Why does the Surah highlight Pharaoh mocking Musa's speech?
questions.answer: To show how arrogant leaders dismiss truth by attacking the messenger instead of answering the message.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about mocking believers or prophets?
questions.answer: Mockery is a sign of arrogance and ends in humiliation and regret in the Hereafter.

questions.text: Who are the only 'close friends' that remain true friends on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: The righteous (muttaqin); other friendships may turn into enmity.

questions.text: What practical lesson can be taken about choosing friends?
questions.answer: Choose companions who help you obey Allah, because friendship affects the Hereafter.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about false beliefs regarding Allah having children?
questions.answer: It rejects such claims and declares Allah far above them, affirming His uniqueness.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah supports making claims about Allah without knowledge.
questions.answer: False. It condemns speaking about Allah without proof.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Allah's knowledge of secret conversations?
questions.answer: Allah knows all secrets, and angels record what people say and do.

questions.text: How does this Surah strengthen belief in accountability even for private speech?
questions.answer: By reminding that private conversations are known and recorded, so nothing is truly hidden.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the suddenness of the Hour?
questions.answer: It can come unexpectedly, so preparation must happen before it arrives.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the joy of Paradise to motivate believers?
questions.answer: With spouses, rejoicing, and honored provision like gold trays and cups, showing comfort and dignity.

questions.text: What is one key contrast the Surah makes between dunya and akhirah?
questions.answer: Dunya's luxury is temporary and can be given to disbelievers, while akhirah is lasting and for the righteous.

questions.text: What does Surah Az-Zukhruf teach about the Prophet's response to persistent rejection?
questions.answer: To turn away from futile arguments, say peace, and trust that people will come to know.

questions.text: How does the Surah encourage patience in da'wah?
questions.answer: By showing that rejection is old, but truth remains, and final outcomes belong to Allah.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about pride in status and lineage?
questions.answer: It does not benefit; many rejected prophets for worldly reasons and were still destroyed.

questions.text: Why does the Surah mention Allah distributing livelihood among people?
questions.answer: To show that worldly ranks are Allah's test and do not equal spiritual rank.

questions.text: What is one misconception about 'success' that this Surah corrects?
questions.answer: That success is luxury; the Surah teaches real success is faith and the Hereafter.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that a person can rely on wealth to escape judgment.
questions.answer: False. Wealth does not protect from Allah's accountability.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about being deluded by ornamentation and display?
questions.answer: It can distract from truth and make people arrogant, leading to spiritual ruin.

questions.text: What is one daily-life application of this Surah regarding consumerism?
questions.answer: Use blessings with gratitude and restraint, and do not let luxury replace worship and obedience.

questions.text: How does the Surah connect shirk with intellectual inconsistency?
questions.answer: People accept Allah as Creator yet worship others, which contradicts logic and justice.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the permanence of relationships in the Hereafter?
questions.answer: Only relationships built on taqwa remain beneficial; others may turn into enemies.

questions.text: What is one way to protect yourself from harmful companionship mentioned in this Surah's message?
questions.answer: Stay connected to Allah's remembrance and choose companions who strengthen faith.

questions.text: How does Surah Az-Zukhruf strengthen tawhid?
questions.answer: By rejecting false claims about Allah, criticizing shirk, and reminding that Allah alone controls guidance and provision.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach respect for Allah when speaking about Him?
questions.answer: By warning against baseless claims and affirming Allah's perfection beyond human imagination.

questions.text: What is one lesson from Pharaoh's downfall for modern leaders?
questions.answer: Do not confuse control and propaganda with truth; arrogance against Allah leads to destruction.

questions.text: Why does the Surah repeatedly expose excuses like 'our fathers did it'?
questions.answer: To push people toward evidence-based faith and personal responsibility.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah encourages a believer to feel hopeless if family or society rejects truth.
questions.answer: False. It teaches patience, peace, and trust that Allah will make truth clear in time.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about worship and gratitude in daily routines?
questions.answer: Even routine acts like travel can become worship when done with remembrance and gratitude.

questions.text: How does this Surah teach the danger of letting emotions decide beliefs?
questions.answer: People reject truth for pride and status; the Surah calls to humility and reason.

questions.text: What is one key reminder about the Hour in Surah Az-Zukhruf?
questions.answer: It comes suddenly, so delaying repentance and obedience is dangerous.

questions.text: How does the Surah encourage believers to define success correctly?
questions.answer: By valuing taqwa and the Hereafter over luxury and status.

questions.text: What is one concluding takeaway about the Quran's status in this Surah?
questions.answer: It is honored with Allah and revealed as clear guidance, so it deserves listening and submission.

questions.text: What is the Surah's strongest call to action for someone living in a luxury-focused culture?
questions.answer: Stay grounded in remembrance, reject shirk and blind following, and prioritize the Hereafter over ornamentation.

questions.text: What does 'Az-Zukhruf' refer to?
questions.answer: Ornaments of gold or worldly luxury.

questions.text: What is the 'Umm al-Kitab' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: The 'Mother of the Book' (the Preserved Tablet/Lauh al-Mahfuz) with Allah.

questions.text: What should one say when mounting a riding beast or vehicle according to this Surah?
questions.answer: 'Exalted is He who has subjected this to us, and we could not have otherwise subdued it'.

questions.text: What was the disbelievers' logic for following their ancestors' religion?
questions.answer: They said, 'We found our fathers upon a religion, and we are following in their footsteps'.

questions.text: How did Ibrahim (AS) dissociate himself from his father's idols?
questions.answer: He said, 'Indeed, I am disassociated from that which you worship, except for He who created me'.

questions.text: What did the disbelievers suggest regarding who the Quran should have been revealed to?
questions.answer: They asked why it wasn't sent down to a 'great man' from one of the two cities (Makkah or Taif).

questions.text: Does Allah divide the mercy of your Lord among people?
questions.answer: Yes, He has apportioned among them their livelihood in the life of this world and raised some in rank.

questions.text: What would Allah have given to the disbelievers if it wouldn't have led all of mankind to become one nation of disbelievers?
questions.answer: Silver roofs for their houses, silver stairs, silver doors, and ornaments of gold.

questions.text: Who is appointed as a companion to the one who turns away from the remembrance of Ar-Rahman?
questions.answer: A devil (Shaytan), who becomes his constant companion.

questions.text: What will the disbeliever say to his devil companion on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: 'Oh, I wish there was between me and you the distance between the east and the west'.

questions.text: What did Pharaoh claim to his people regarding the kingdom of Egypt?
questions.answer: He said, 'Does not the kingdom of Egypt belong to me, and these rivers flowing beneath me?'.

questions.text: How did Pharaoh describe Musa (AS) in a mocking way?
questions.answer: As one who is 'contemptible' and can 'hardly make himself clear' in speech.

questions.text: What is the status of 'close friends' on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: They will be enemies to one another, except for the righteous (Muttaqin).

questions.text: What will the dwellers of Hell call out to 'Malik' (the gatekeeper of Hell)?
questions.answer: 'O Malik, let your Lord put an end to us!' and he will say, 'Indeed, you are to remain'.

questions.text: If Ar-Rahman had a son, what would the Prophet (SAW) be the first to do?
questions.answer: He would be the first to worship him, but Allah is far above such claims.

questions.text: Who knows the 'Secret and the Private Conversation' of people?
questions.answer: Allah, and His messengers (angels) are with them recording.

questions.text: Is the one who created the heavens and earth the same as the One who is God in heaven and God on earth?
questions.answer: Yes, He is the Wise, the Knowing.

questions.text: What will happen to the 'Hour' (Day of Judgment)?
questions.answer: It will come suddenly while they perceive not.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the people of Paradise and their joy?
questions.answer: They enter Paradise with their spouses, rejoicing, with golden trays and cups circulated among them.

questions.text: What is the final instruction to the Prophet regarding the disbelievers?
questions.answer: Turn away from them and say 'Peace', for they will eventually come to know.
